values: Install and launch the Android emulator build, then fill Test Runs row by row. Every failure gets an Error Log ID and gets linked back to Stories.
Stories is canonical. Do not create parallel trackers; update this workbook as tests, fixes, and retests happen.
As a user, I want the app to open into my last saved todo state so I can continue without setup.
On launch, todos load from SQLite, settings and Google auth load from storage, default settings are normalized when missing, and the UI becomes usable after both load paths finish.
App.tsx load effects; src/storage/todoDatabase.ts; src/storage/appSettingsStore.ts; src/google/googleAuthStore.ts
As a returning user, I want older todo data to migrate into the current database without losing search.

If legacy AsyncStorage todos exist and SQLite is empty, the app migrates them, creates search columns and FTS table, rebuilds stale search data, and removes the legacy key only after a successful migration.
As a user, I want date labels and overdue state to stay correct as days change.
The app refreshes date status after midnight and when returning active so Today, Tomorrow, Overdue, and remaining-day labels update without a manual restart.
As a user with reminders, I want scheduled notifications to recover when the app resumes.
On launch and app active transitions, todo alarms are reconciled against current todos and throttled to avoid repeated sync churn.
As a mobile user, I want taps and state changes to feel responsive without being noisy.
Major interactions call the shared subtle haptic helper and gracefully no-op if the platform haptics call fails.
As a user, I want clear empty states so I understand whether I have no todos, no active todos, or no search matches.
The list shows distinct empty text for no items, no active items when done items are hidden, item search mode, saved-list search mode, and unmatched search terms.
As a user, I want each todo row to show the useful compact task information.
Rows show checkbox, title, optional content preview, visible meta tags, pin marker, color styling, done styling, and search highlights without layout overlap.
As a user, I want pinned todos to remain visually important and sorted ahead of normal todos.
Pinned todos persist in the todo model, render with pinned styling, affect row width safely, and load ordered before unpinned todos from SQLite.
As a user, I want to complete and reopen todos quickly from the row checkbox.
Tapping the checkbox toggles done state, records undo unless suppressed, updates SQLite, updates reminders, hides the row when done items are hidden, and supports reopen.
As a user, I want swipe actions for common row work without opening a separate screen.
Swiping exposes delete and done actions on one side and the todo settings/menu action on the other, closes other open swipe rows, and keeps pending-delete rows inert.
As a user, I want to tap a row and edit the title and notes in a focused detail sheet.

A tap opens the compact detail modal unless selection or swipe state intercepts; save writes normalized title/content/tags, records undo, updates SQLite, syncs alarms, highlights the edited row, and closes the modal.
As a user, I want to long press todos to select multiple items for batch actions.
A long press outside the checkbox-copy zone enters selection mode, selected rows are highlighted, tapping rows toggles selection, and the header changes to selected-count actions.
src/components/TodoRow.tsx; App.tsx toggleTodoSelection; App.tsx app header selection actions
As a user, I want to complete or reopen selected todos together.
The selected header action marks every selected non-pending todo done or active, records one undo entry, exits selection mode, and preserves invalid rows safely.
As a user, I want to delete selected todos with confirmation.
Deleting selected todos opens a confirmation, records one undo entry for deletable rows, moves them to deleted items, cancels alarms, exits selection, and does not double-record per row.
As a user, I want to group todos by status, priority, date, or list so I can scan a view by context.
Grouped views render section headers, counts, rows, empty zero-item list groups where appropriate, and support group-specific row estimates without visual jumps.
As a user, I want to collapse sections that are not relevant right now.
Tapping a group header toggles collapsed state with persistence in settings; quick-nav double tap can toggle all grouped sections.
As a user, I want to add a todo directly into a visible group or section.
Section add controls open the create drawer with filters derived from that section, including list/date/priority/tag/repeat context when creatable.
Open item menu from a todo row
As a user, I want to open a menu for one todo so I can edit its metadata without entering the detail sheet.

Swiping the row toward the menu side or pressing the row menu action opens the bottom menu, highlights and scrolls the target row above the sheet, closes other swipe rows, and keeps pending-delete rows inert.
src/components/TodoRow.tsx openTodoMenu; App.tsx openMenuForTodoAction; App.tsx requestTodoMenuTargetScroll
Edit one todo's lists, tags, date, and priority
As a user, I want the item menu to change the selected todo's list, tags, date, reminder, repeat, and priority without changing my current view filters.
When an item is the menu target, list/tag/priority/date rows update that todo's filters, persist the todo, sync alarms when relevant, record undo, and leave global selected filters intact.
App.tsx hasTodoEditTargets; App.tsx updateCurrentTodoTargetFilters; App.tsx bottomMenuItems
As a user, I want to pin or unpin one todo or selected todos from the menu.
The menu pin row reflects whether all targets are pinned, toggles only the active item or selected set, persists pin state, updates sorting, records undo, and keeps the menu context stable.
App.tsx todoPinActionLabel; App.tsx updateTodoPinnedForIds; App.tsx bottomMenuItems
Delete item targets from the menu
As a user, I want the item menu to delete the current todo or selected todos with undo protection.
Delete from the menu routes through the same pending-delete, deleted-items, alarm-cancel, confirmation where needed, and undo flows as swipe and bulk delete.
App.tsx bottomMenuItems deleteAction; App.tsx deleteTodo; App.tsx deleteSelectedTodos
As a user, I want to create a new todo using another todo's settings so repeated data entry is fast.

The create-from-settings hold/action opens the create drawer with the source todo's list/date/priority/tag/reminder context, shows the cue while holding, and does not accidentally select or complete the source row.
src/components/TodoRow.tsx handleRowLongPressStateChange; App.tsx openCreateDrawerWithFilters; App.tsx createFromSettings cue
As a user, I want a fast new-todo drawer from the bottom add button.
The add action closes menus, exits selection, seeds remembered or current filters, clears stale picker state, focuses on a clean draft, and opens the drawer.
App.tsx openCreateDrawer; App.tsx bottom nav Add todo
As a user, I want saving a new todo to create it immediately and keep it visible.

Submitting a non-empty title normalizes text/content/tags, creates the todo, records undo, persists to SQLite, syncs alarm, clears item search, reveals or adjusts filters so the new row is visible, highlights it, and remembers creatable filters.
As a user, I want the create drawer to remember useful filters without trapping me in impossible contexts.
The drawer remembers valid create filters, derives defaults from current list/filter context when possible, and falls back to the last remembered filters when the current view is not creatable.
As a user, I want to assign a new todo to a list from the create drawer.
The list picker shows current top-level lists in configured order plus a no-list option, selecting a list stores one list value, and tapping an active list control clears it.
As a user, I want date shortcuts and custom dates while creating a todo.
The date picker supports Today, Tomorrow, This Week, Next Week, Later, Someday, custom date, selected custom sorting, clear date, and date label display modes.
As a user, I want to add a reminder time when creating a todo.
The Reminder control opens a time picker, defaults to a valid time, stores a normalized HH:MM reminder, supports clearing time, and preserves any selected repeat setting.
App.tsx openCreateReminderModal; App.tsx confirmReminderTime; src/components/ReminderTimeModal.tsx; src/reminders.ts
As a user, I want repeating todos to be daily, weekly, monthly, yearly, or none.
The repeat modal and weekly/monthly shortcuts update encoded reminder state, preserve reminder time, and optionally pair a custom date with repeat selection.
App.tsx openCreateRepeatModal; App.tsx confirmCreateRepeat; src/components/RepeatReminderModal.tsx; src/reminders.ts
As a user, I want a new todo to have a priority or no priority.
The create priority picker offers High, Medium, Low, and None; None clears priority, non-None stores exactly one priority, and active priority can be cleared by tapping the toolbar control.
As a user, I want to tag a new todo using my configured or existing tags.
The tag picker shows normalized custom and existing tags, toggles tags without duplicates, merges active tag filters into the created todo, and can clear active draft tags.
App.tsx availableTodoTags; App.tsx toggleCreateDraftTag; src/todos.ts normalizeTodoTags
As a user, I want to create a pinned todo directly.
The create drawer pin toggle changes draft pinned state and the saved todo persists as pinned.
As a user, I want active create drawer chips to be easy to clear.
Tapping an already-open active date/list/priority/tag control clears that part of the draft or returns to the main input as coded.
Create from an existing todo settings
As a user, I want to long press a todo checkbox area to start a new todo with the same settings.
A long press on the checkbox zone shows the create cue, opens the create drawer on release, copies list/date/priority/reminder/tags/pin settings, and does not enter select mode.
As a user, I want to edit a todo title and longer content safely.
The detail sheet uses a compact bottom modal, enforces a non-empty title, normalizes content line endings, saves only changed data, records undo, persists, highlights, and closes.
App.tsx active todo detail modal; App.tsx saveActiveTodoDetail
Detail save disabled for invalid state
As a user, I want invalid or unchanged detail edits to avoid accidental saves.
The detail save path returns early when no active todo exists, the draft cannot be edited, or the title/content/tags are unchanged or invalid.
As a user, I want detail edits to include tags.
The detail draft tracks normalized tags, toggles existing/custom tags without duplicates, saves tags with title/content, and persists to SQLite.
App.tsx activeTodoDetailDraftTags; App.tsx toggleActiveTodoDetailDraftTag; App.tsx saveActiveTodoDetail
As a user, I want a full filter screen for common filtering without digging through a drawer.

A single filter-nav tap shows filtered results when filters are active; a quick double tap opens the full-screen filter config with lists, tags, priority, date, sort, group, meta tags, clear controls, and a result count footer.
As a user, I want selected filter values to broaden within a family and combine across families.
Selected values in one family use OR semantics, different families combine as AND, and the resulting list updates using todoMatchesFilters.
App.tsx todoMatchesAnyOptionalFilter; App.tsx todoMatchesFilters; src/storage/appSettingsStore.ts todoMatchesSelectedListFilters
As a user, I want to pin filters as required conditions when optional matching is too broad.
Required filters are kept in a separate settings lane, pruned to selected values, must match every visible todo, and do not apply while editing selected todos.
App.tsx requiredFilters; App.tsx todoMatchesRequiredFilters; App.tsx toggleRequiredFilterValue
As a user, I want to exclude unwanted values from a view.
Avoided list/date/priority/tag/repeating filters exclude matching todos, clear from selected filters where needed, and are included in saved presets and backups.
App.tsx avoidedFilters; App.tsx todoMatchesAvoidedFilters; src/storage/appSettingsStore.ts; src/google/driveBackup.ts
As a user, I want parent lists and child list sections to behave predictably.
Selecting a parent list matches todos directly in the parent or child labels where subsection logic applies; selecting a subsection focuses that subsection and can use subsection display settings.
As a user, I want date filters that cover overdue, dated, relative, later, someday, and custom dates.
Date filters normalize relative labels, support custom ISO dates, resolve due/overdue/later logic from the todo's creation anchor, and show display labels according to the current date label mode.
src/dates.ts; src/todoDates.ts; App.tsx date filtering helpers
As a user, I want a filter that finds repeating todos without corrupting reminder data.
Repeating items is stored as a filter-only sentinel in reminder filters, normalized separately from actual reminder time/repeat values, counted and cleared correctly, and can be selected or avoided.
As a user, I want to filter and sort by priority.
Priority filter choices are High, Medium, Low, and None; filter colors and priority sort rank respect those values.
As a user, I want to filter todos by tags.
The filter config and drawer show available tags from custom, active, and deleted todos; selected tags match todo tags and can be removed or cleared.
Active filter list and clear controls
As a user, I want to see and remove active filters quickly.
The filters menu shows active filters, individual clear buttons, section clears, and clear-all behavior that resets selected, required, and avoided filters without leaving stale state.
As a user, I want the filter screen to reopen where I left it.
Expanded sections and scroll offset persist through settings normalization and save/load.
As a user, I want to sort todos by newest, oldest, A to Z, priority, priority then date, or date.
Sort choices update global state or active single-list display settings, persist, and order todos with pinned items first.
As a user, I want to group by none, priority, date, list, or status.
Group mode updates global or active list display settings, persists, renders expected sections, and can be cleared back to None.
As a user, I want to control which compact metadata tags show on rows.
Date, list, tags, priority, and created date visibility can be toggled globally or through presets; row rendering hides disabled kinds and formats a summary.
As a user, I want date tags to show exact dates, remaining time, or both.
Date label style cycles exact, remaining, and both in filter config/settings and affects date menu/tag display consistently.
As a user, I want the bottom drawer to be reachable and dismissible on a phone.
The list/menu drawer opens at a half-height ratio, includes one-handed scroll offsets, supports back/close gestures, closes on backdrop, and keeps submenu back labels correct.
App.tsx list menu constants; handleMenuDismissGesture; bottomMenuItems render
Main menu is the hub for view editing
As a user, I want the bottom menu to be the central place to edit what I am looking at.
With no item target, the main menu shows Lists, Tags, Priority, Date, Sort, Group, Meta tags, active Filters, Clear filters, Settings, and Save list affordances with current counts or summaries.
App.tsx bottomMenuItems; App.tsx menuMode main rows; App.tsx canSaveListMenuPreset
As a user, I want menu submenus to be easy to enter, back out of, and dismiss.

Opening a submenu changes the visible rows, the back affordance returns to the main menu, Android back follows modal/submenu priority, and pull-down gestures either go back or close depending on depth.
App.tsx goBackInMenu; App.tsx goBackFromMenuDismissGesture; App.tsx handleSubmenuBackGestureStateChange
Sort group and meta-tag menu editing
As a user, I want to change sorting, grouping, and row metadata from the menu.

Sort, Group, and Meta tags submenus show the current selection, apply changes immediately to the visible view, update settings/list display state where appropriate, and record undo for meaningful changes.
Active filters menu and clear actions
As a user, I want to review and remove active filters from the menu.

The Filters submenu lists selected, required, and avoided filters separately, supports removing one active filter, clearing a section, and clearing all filters without crossing list/preset boundaries.
Save current view as a list
As a user, I want to save my current filters and display choices as a reusable list.
Saving captures selected, required, avoided filters, list order, sort, group, meta-tag visibility, label, and createdAt into a StoredMenuPreset.
As a user, I want a saved list to restore its complete view.

Applying a saved list restores filters, required filters, avoided filters, list order, sort, group, list display settings for single-list presets, meta tags, title identity, and selected quick-nav state.
As a user, I want to update an existing saved list after adjusting its view.
Editing/open saved list update paths detect meaningful changes, record undo, overwrite the preset view definition, and return to the saved-list menu without applying a wrong list.
As a user, I want to remove saved lists I no longer need.
Deleting a saved list records undo, removes quick-nav assignments for that preset, clears open preset state, and resets the current view if the deleted preset was active.
As a user, I want hidden keywords to help find saved lists and lists without cluttering labels.
Long-press/edit keyword flows store normalized hidden keywords for presets and lists, use them in search scoring, and keep them out of normal visible row labels.
As a user, I want saved lists to contain focused subsections.

A preset section stores its own filters, required filters, avoided filters, sort, group, list order, meta tags, label, and createdAt, can be added without duplicate view definitions, and can be deleted with undo.
As a user, I want selecting a saved section to switch to that section's view.
Applying a section restores that section's filters/display settings, anchors the parent preset as open, and closes the menu.
As a user, I want the bottom quick navbar to jump to saved lists.
Tapping a populated slot applies its preset, closes filter/settings surfaces, updates open slot state, blurs search, and leaves empty slots disabled.
As a user, I want to preview what a quick navbar item represents.
Long press on a populated slot shows a transient detail card with title and summary lines, and releasing clears it.
Quick navbar double tap group toggle
As a user, I want double tapping the current quick list to collapse or expand grouped sections.
A second tap within the configured interval increments the toggle-all request while still applying the preset normally.
As a user, I want the header and selected state to reflect the exact active quick item.
The header title and selected quick slot prefer explicit open slot/preset identity over incidental list-label matches, and search mode does not show selected quick-nav state.
Preset menu lists saved views and sections
As a user, I want a preset menu that shows every saved list and its saved sections in one place.
The Presets menu shows the list-filter editor row, the latest-list quick-apply row, each saved list with its summary, each section indented under its list, and a save-current row when applicable.
App.tsx bottomMenuItems menuMode presets; App.tsx MenuPresetSwipeRow
Edit a saved list from the preset menu
As a user, I want to edit a saved list's filters, sort, group, and metadata display.

Pressing Edit on a saved list applies it into the menu editor, switches to the main menu, lets the user change list/tag/date/priority/sort/group/meta rows, and saves back to the same preset only when the user taps Save list.
App.tsx openMenuPresetEditor; App.tsx updateEditingMenuPreset; App.tsx listMenuPresetSaveLabel
Save changes to the currently open preset
As a user, I want edits made while viewing a saved preset to be saved back intentionally.

When a saved list is open and the view differs from the preset definition, the menu exposes Save list; saving updates the existing preset, records undo, clears editing mode, and keeps the preset selected.
App.tsx openMenuPresetHasChanges; App.tsx saveOpenMenuPreset; App.tsx canSaveListMenuPreset
As a user, I want a saved list to contain sections so I can keep related mini-views under one preset.

The preset row add-section action snapshots the current filters/sort/group/meta into a unique section label, prevents duplicate sections, and applying a section restores that section's view without overwriting the parent preset.
App.tsx addPresetSection; App.tsx applyPresetSection; App.tsx presetSectionMatchesState
As a user, I want to remove saved lists or sections from the preset menu without breaking other presets.
Swiping a saved list or section exposes delete, records undo, removes only that preset or section, clears open preset state if needed, and leaves list labels/todo membership untouched.
App.tsx MenuPresetSwipeRow; App.tsx removePresetSection; App.tsx delete preset handlers
Edit preset and list search keywords
As a user, I want saved lists and list labels to have hidden search keywords so I can find them by my own terms.

Keyword edit prompts support preset keywords, list keywords, list renames, and navbar names; saves normalize whitespace, reject duplicates or empty labels where needed, persist settings, and record undo.
App.tsx openPresetSearchKeywordPrompt; App.tsx openSettingsListKeywordPrompt; App.tsx commitSearchKeywords
As a user, I want to quickly reapply the latest saved list after saving or editing it.
When a latest saved list exists, the Presets menu exposes a Latest list submenu row that applies the newest preset and closes the menu without forcing the user through the full saved-list list.
App.tsx latestMenuPreset; App.tsx menuMode presetsQuickApply; App.tsx applyMenuPreset
As a user, I want the menu to make it clear whether I am editing items, the current view, or a saved preset.

When a todo or selected todos are targeted, menu rows update those items; when no items are targeted, rows update current filters/view; when editing/opening a preset, save actions update the preset deliberately without mutating unrelated list labels.
App.tsx hasTodoEditTargets; App.tsx activeTodoMenuId; App.tsx editingMenuPresetId; App.tsx openMenuPresetId
As a user, I want the search button and search field to enter search mode predictably.
Focusing the header search or tapping search opens the search tab, restores the relevant scroll offset, and clears notification reveal state.
As a user, I want search to find saved lists and list sections.
List search mode ranks saved lists and list menu rows by label and hidden keywords, shows counts, supports collapsed sections, and does not require an item search.
As a user, I want item search across title, content, and tags.
Item search uses normalized AND terms, queries SQLite when needed, caches results by query, and displays matching todo rows.
src/storage/todoDatabase.ts searchTodosInDatabase; App.tsx itemSearchState; src/components/TodoRow.tsx highlights
As a user, I want matched terms highlighted in the row title and content preview.
Search terms normalize diacritics and repeated terms, highlight title/content ranges, and merge overlapping highlight ranges.
As a user, I want switching search modes to keep my place.
Preset and item search modes save/restore their own scroll offsets and apply the correct one-handed default offset on first open.
App.tsx searchScrollOffsetsByModeRef; restoreSearchScroll; useLayoutEffect search mode
As a user, I want search nav shortcuts to clear or focus quickly.
The search nav focuses search on repeated press, clears the query on quick double tap, and long press expands/collapses all search sections.
As a user, I want settings to open as a focused preferences panel and close back to the list.
Opening settings closes menus/filter config, exits selection, resets expanded transient sections, clears icon pickers, and shows undo in the settings header.
As a user, I want to configure date label style and overdue count from Settings.
Settings Date labels expands/collapses, cycles label style, toggles overdue count, records undo, and persists both settings.
App.tsx settings Date labels section; toggleDateLabelDisplayMode; toggleShowOverdueMetaTags
As a user, I want to hide done todos without deleting them.
The Done items setting toggles hideDoneTodos, records undo, persists, updates empty-state language, and keeps done todos saved.
App.tsx toggleHideDoneTodos; settings Done items section; src/storage/appSettingsStore.ts
As a user, I want Settings to show deleted todos for later recovery.
Deleted items section shows count, empty state, deleted rows with title/content preview/time, and opens a deleted item detail card.
App.tsx settings Deleted items section; App.tsx openDeletedTodoDetailModal
As a user, I want to restore a deleted todo with its data intact.
Restore removes the item from deleted settings, re-adds it to active todos, persists settings and SQLite, syncs any alarm, records undo, and closes the deleted detail when relevant.
As a user, I want to permanently remove a deleted todo only after confirmation.
Permanent delete prompts confirmation, records undo on confirmation, removes the deleted snapshot from settings, and closes the deleted detail if open.
As a user, I want to add a new quick navbar item from Settings.

Adding a navbar item creates or reuses a matching list, creates a saved list preset for that list, appends quick-nav assignment and icon, persists all related settings, and rejects duplicates or over-limit additions.
As a user, I want to rename the label shown for a quick navbar item.
Editing a navbar item updates the assigned preset label or creates a concrete saved preset for a virtual/list-backed item, records undo, and persists quick-nav assignment changes.
As a user, I want to choose a quick navbar icon.
Icon picker opens for a navbar row, chosen icons normalize against Material Community names, reset uses the slot default, and persisted icon names keep quick nav and Settings aligned.
As a user, I want to reorder quick navbar items.
Tapping two reorder handles swaps quick-nav preset assignments and icons, records undo, clears stale open/held quick slot state, and persists the new order.
As a user, I want to remove a quick navbar item without deleting all user data.
Swiping/removing a navbar item deletes only the quick-nav assignment/icon slot, updates open/held slot numbers, records undo, and persists.
As a user, I want to add a list/category from Settings.
Adding a list trims and formats the label, rejects duplicates across the full tree, prepends the list, records undo, persists, and clears the input.
Rename list and edit list keywords
As a user, I want to rename a list and add hidden keywords without breaking existing todos or saved views.

Renaming a list updates selected/required/avoided filters, create filters, colors, menu presets, preset sections, active todos, deleted todos, and collapsed search state in one persisted snapshot while rejecting duplicates.
As a user, I want deleting a list to be safe and explicit.

Deleting an unused list removes it immediately; deleting a used list prompts with affected count, then removes that list and child labels from todos, deleted todos, filters, presets, sections, last-create filters, and colors.
As a user, I want the list menu order to be alphabetical or manual.
Settings list order segmented control toggles alphabetical/manual, records undo, persists, and manual reorder swaps list rows only when manual ordering is active in code paths that allow it.
App.tsx settings List menu order; App.tsx swapSettingsListItems
As a user, I want to add, rename, and delete custom tags.
Tag add/edit/delete normalizes labels, rejects duplicates, records undo, persists custom tags, and propagates renames/deletes across active todos, deleted todos, and draft tag state.
As a user, I want to color lists, dates, and priorities.
Colors section groups colorable items, swatch selection stores normalized hex or no-color values, records undo, updates row/tag themes, and persists settings.
As a developer testing the app, I want seed data that exercises lists, presets, filters, reminders, and grouping.
In dev builds only, Settings can add deterministic seed lists/presets/todos and clear only seed data without deleting user-created data.
As a user, I want a quick undo button after risky actions.
Undoable actions create bounded history entries, show a toast where appropriate, allow dismiss, and restore the captured snapshot on undo.
App.tsx UndoSnapshot; createUndoHistoryEntry; recordUndo; restoreUndoSnapshot; undo toast render
As a user, I want persistent undo/redo access from the header.
Header and settings undo buttons show count, switch to redo when redo history exists, disable at zero count, and execute the correct history action.
App.tsx undoHistory; redoHistory; undoLastChange; redoLastChange; header render
As a user, I want a filter editing session to produce one undo entry instead of one per tap.
Filter config/menu changes begin an undo batch, mark it pending through mutations, and flush one entry when the filter surface closes.
Undo coverage for destructive and settings changes
As a user, I want destructive and major settings changes to be recoverable.
Create, edit, delete, restore, complete, pin, colors, settings list/navbar/tag, preset, backup restore, and filter changes record undo where code paths mutate user data.
As a user, I want reminder notifications to be scheduled from todo reminder state.
Todos with valid reminder times schedule notifications, deleted/done/no-reminder todos cancel or avoid stale alarms, and alarm reconciliation keeps schedules aligned.
As an Android user, I want the app to guide me when exact alarms are unavailable.
Android exact alarm checks use the native module when available, open exact alarm settings or app settings when needed, and surface fallback notification behavior without silent failure.
As a user, I want tapping a reminder notification to reveal the related todo.
Notification responses resolve a todo id, update app context enough to make that todo visible, scroll/highlight it, and clear reveal state after use.
As a user, I want completing a repeating todo to move it to the next due date instead of simply disappearing.
Completing a repeating todo computes the next date from the repeat interval, schedules a short completion feedback state, updates the todo, and preserves undo trust.
As a user, I want a one-tap backup action in the bottom nav.
The cloud button is disabled while busy or unconfigured, authenticates if needed, creates a Drive appData backup snapshot, updates backup status, and includes todos plus settings.
App.tsx bottom nav backup; App.tsx backupToGoogleDrive; src/google/driveBackup.ts
As a user, I want Settings to manage backup, restore, and backup browsing.
Backup Settings shows connection state, config warnings, auto-backup switch, manual backup, restore, show backups, disconnect, and status text.
App.tsx settings Backup section; App.tsx performGoogleDriveAction
As a user, I want enabled auto backup to snapshot changes without repeated prompts.
When enabled with valid auth, changes to the auto-backup state key debounce into a backup, avoid concurrent runs, and remember failed state to prevent loops.
As a user, I want to choose from multiple backup slots.
Backup/restore/manage uses a modal picker, shows filled and next-empty slots for backup, disables empty restore slots, supports cancel/done, and records file metadata.
As a user, I want to remove old backup slots safely.
In manage mode, swiping/deleting a saved slot confirms, deletes only that Drive file, refreshes the slot list, and updates status.
As a user, I want restore to replace the local app state with a chosen backup.

Restore downloads the selected backup, records undo, replaces SQLite todos, restores settings including lists/presets/colors/nav/meta/deleted/filter state, reconciles alarms, and updates restore timestamp.
As a user, I want to disconnect Drive auth without deleting local data.
Disconnect signs out native Google on Android when possible, clears stored auth, keeps todos/settings intact, updates status, and handles busy state.
As a user, I want todos to persist locally with all fields intact.
SQLite stores id, text, content, tags, done, pinned, createdAt, filters, normalized search columns, and FTS rows; upsert/update/delete paths keep search in sync.
As a user, I want all app customization to survive restart.

AsyncStorage settings normalize defaults and preserve filters, list tree, saved lists, sections, quick nav, colors, deleted todos, meta tags, backup state, done visibility, collapsed groups, sort, and group.
As a user, I want Google tokens stored separately from backups and clearable on disconnect.
Google auth persists through SecureStore, is not included in backup payloads, refreshes or clears expired tokens, and can be removed independently.
As a user, I want older and newer backups to restore safely.
Backup payload creation and normalization include current settings shape, clone user-owned arrays/trees, support legacy slot behavior, normalize invalid modes, and preserve preset sections.
As a tester, I want dev and prod builds to use separate identities where needed.
